title: Expressions should not be too complex
description: The complexity of an expression is defined by the number of &&, ||, and condition ? ifTrue : ifFalse operators it contains. A single expression's complexity should not become too high to keep the code readable. With the default threshold value of 3, expressions containing more than 3 logical operators should be simplified.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.reasoning: Detects expressions with more than 3 logical operators (&&, ||) which reduce code readability. The pattern matches expressions with 4 or more operators. Exclusions prevent false positives in test assertions and logging where complex expressions are acceptable for verification purposes.
